_comment: Ground truth annotation — CONSORT checklist for Boldt retracted CPB priming article

article.title: Cardiopulmonary Bypass Priming Using a High Dose of a Balanced Hydroxyethyl Starch Versus an Albumin-Based Priming Strategy
article.authors: Joachim Boldt, Stephan Suttner, Christian Brosch, Andreas Lehmann, Kerstin Röhm, Andinet Mengistu
article.journal: Journal of Thoracic and Cardiovascular Surgery

study_type.notes: Small RCT (n=50) comparing CPB priming solutions (balanced HES vs albumin-based). RETRACTED article by Joachim Boldt whose research has been subject to fraud investigations and multiple retractions. Expected to show poor CONSORT reporting as negative control.

1a.notes: Title identifies study as randomized: 'the CPB circuit was prospectively and randomly primed with either...'

1b.notes: Structured abstract with Background, Methods, Results, Conclusion sections

2a.notes: Background provides rationale: unclear which priming solution is optimal for CPB; prior studies mentioned

2b.notes: Study objective stated: 'to evaluate the influence of high-volume priming with a modern balanced hydroxyethyl starch (HES) preparation on coagulation, inflammation, and organ function compared with an albumin-based CPB priming regimen'

3a.notes: Trial design described: 'prospectively and randomly primed'; two parallel groups (HES vs. albumin); specific allocation not detailed

3b.notes: No mention of protocol amendments or changes after trial commencement

4a.notes: Eligibility criteria: 'patients undergoing coronary artery bypass grafting'; age and specific inclusion/exclusion criteria minimally described

4b.notes: Settings not clearly specified; appears to be single-center study but location/institution not stated

5.notes: Interventions detailed: intervention group (n=25) received 1500 mL of 6% HES 130/0.42 in balanced electrolyte solution; control group (n=25) received 500 mL of 5% human albumin plus 1000 mL 0.9% saline solution

6a.notes: Primary outcomes: inflammation (IL-6, IL-10), endothelial damage (ICAM-1), kidney function (β-GST, NGAL), coagulation (ROTEM), platelet function (Multiplate analyzer)

6b.notes: No mention of changes to outcomes after trial commenced

7a.notes: Sample size calculation not described; 50 patients total appears small and unjustified; no power analysis mentioned

7b.notes: No mention of interim analyses or stopping rules

8a.notes: Method used to generate random allocation sequence not described; only stated that CPB 'was prospectively and randomly primed'

8b.notes: Type of randomization not specified; no mention of stratification, blocking, or randomization scheme

9.notes: Allocation concealment not described; no mention of methods to conceal treatment assignment

10.notes: Who generated the sequence, enrolled participants, and assigned interventions not clearly described

11a.notes: No mention of blinding of patients or staff; appears to be open-label given nature of intervention (priming solution)

11b.notes: Not applicable; open intervention design, no masking of appearance between groups

12a.notes: Statistical methods mentioned: 'P=0.0003', 'P≤0.0002', 'P≤0.00004', 'P≤0.004'; t-tests or Mann-Whitney U tests implied but not explicitly named

12b.notes: No subgroup analyses or additional analyses described

13a.notes: No participant flow diagram; no CONSORT flow chart showing enrollment, allocation, follow-up

13b.notes: No losses or exclusions after randomization reported; no information on adherence or dropout

14a.notes: No dates for recruitment period or follow-up period specified

14b.notes: No statement of why trial ended or was stopped

15.notes: No baseline characteristics table; no demographic or clinical characteristics of groups reported; groups presumably comparable but not described

16.notes: No description of intention-to-treat vs per-protocol analysis; number analyzed per group not explicitly stated (appears to be 25 per group but not confirmed)

17a.notes: Results presented with p-values: IL-6, IL-10, ICAM-1, β-GST, NGAL, ROTEM parameters, platelet function all reported with statistical comparisons

17b.notes: Results presented as mean values and p-values; no confidence intervals or effect sizes reported

18.notes: No subgroup analyses or sensitivity analyses described

19.notes: No systematic reporting of adverse events or complications; some outcomes (e.g., base excess, clotting measures) might reflect harm but not labeled as such

20.notes: No discussion of limitations; paper does not address potential biases or sources of imprecision

21.notes: No discussion of generalizability or external validity

22.notes: Discussion minimal; brief conclusions but limited interpretation in context of prior evidence

23.notes: No trial registration number provided; pre-dates common registration but should have been disclosed

24.notes: No statement regarding protocol availability or supplementary materials

25.notes: No funding sources or conflicts of interest disclosed; no statement of funder role
